name: OData verbose error leaks parser type + stack trace
description: Sends a malformed $filter expression to an OData collection and checks the error response. When the service answers with a detailed error — the internal OData parser exception type (Microsoft.OData.ODataException) and a stack trace — it discloses implementation internals (framework, version-specific call paths) that help an attacker fingerprint the stack and craft further injection. Verbose errors are the OData analogue of a leaked developer exception page. The predicate looks for the OData exception type / stacktrace markers, so a terse RFC-compliant error body (code + generic message only) does not trip it.

vulnerability.remediation: Return terse, RFC-compliant OData errors in production — a `code` and a generic `message`, never the exception type, inner error, or stack trace. ASP.NET Core OData: don't enable detailed errors outside Development (`ODataOptions` / `IncludeErrorDetail` gated to the Development environment), and add a global exception handler that maps parser failures to a plain 400. Verify with `curl -k 'https://host/odata/Orders?$filter=bogus'` — the body should not contain `ODataException`, `stacktrace`, or a namespace-qualified type.
